verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II single-pass TM glycoprotein with its catalytic domain facing the extracellular/luminal space. Its canonical home is the trans-Golgi, where it performs glycoconjugate biosynthesis. However, a well-documented PM surface pool exists: it is described as 'mainly localized at the plasma membrane' in certain contexts (PubMed:1714903), and the cell surface form mediates cell–cell and cell–matrix recognition. PM surface expression is most robustly documented on spermatogenic cells (sperm surface GalTase is an externally-oriented integral PM component mediating sperm-egg recognition), representing a germline/tissue-restricted surface display. Its own TM anchor supports the surface form in that compartment. Dual Golgi/PM localization with strong tissue restriction to germline/sperm cells qualifies as contextual.
